rows.title: Engineering inputs needed for Retrievable Bridge Plug animation (Focus Digital)
rows.description:
Hi Jake! Following up on the Focus Digital meeting with Brett. To kick off Week 1 of production, we need three inputs from our side:

STEP/CAD file of the wellhead type to be used
Standard procedure sequence that sets the retrievable bridge plug
Confirmation of the scenarios to be animated (normal deployment, worn/unretrievable replacement, alternative routine)

Could you point me to the right engineering contact(s) so I can coordinate the delivery of the assets? Quote is $4,800 total, with a 4–6 week timeline once we hand the files over to Focus Digital.
(Also doubling as an OMP test.. flag anything that doesn't render right.)

rows.title: NCR #82007431 - Process Loss - Chord Hoff
rows.description:
Event: Deployment Audit Flowback HWO on cross All 6 trees cranked full open for deployment, NO 1/4 turn off. Had to hand off of 2 to get them to move.

Immediate action: Set valves in proper position

Follow-ups: Assigned to Jaelen Maslowski on 5/26/26, Due 5/28/26 Work with both crews to understand that valves must be backed off 1/4 turn after full open or full close - by Timothy Dibben on 5/26/26 Assigned to Jaelen Maslowski on 5/25/26 Is this how we are transporting valves ? New process? - by Bryce Christoffersen on 5/25/26

rows.title: OKRs and Projects tracking/ R&D
rows.description:
We need to have a discussion around how we are managing parent OKRs and how we can view OKRs through a clear, filterable structure.

We also need to discuss project management for active projects, including R&D projects. This should include how we track the required quality forms and documentation that are part of the product assessment process.

For viable product assessments, we need to make sure the process includes key items such as:

Economic evaluation
Risk assessment
Quality review forms
Product viability review
Required approvals
Next steps and ownership

The main point is that I do not want us to simply request something from without first working through the proper funnel of how this should function for our business. We need to define the structure, logic, required steps, and desired reporting before we ask them to build or configure anything.

This needs to include how OKRs roll up to parent OKRs, how projects are linked to those OKRs, how project status is tracked, and how required forms or assessments are tied into the project workflow.

The goal is to make sure OKRs, projects, and product assessments are structured in a way that gives us clear visibility, accountability, and follow-through. If we map this correctly up front, we can create a system that supports how we actually manage priorities, evaluate new products, track R&D, and move projects from idea to execution.

Please begin thinking through how this should work in your areas so we can have a productive discussion @Mercileidy Jimenez @Tim Dibben @Andrew Emmel

classification.notes: No new user request rows, bug reports, Fix-It posts, comments, objective changes, org chart changes, NCR/KPI/OKR audit events, or messages since checkpoint. Activity is scheduled notification email delivery, dashboard presence, and one Andrew dashboard preference update.
